name: Smoked Stuffed Artichokes
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim tops and leaves of artichokes with scissors, cutting off the top third and tips of the remaining leaves to remove "spikes."
2. Put artichokes in a kettle of boiling salted water and simmer for 35 to 40 minutes or until a leaf can be pulled out easily. Drain, refresh under cold running water, and let stand upside down until cool.
3. To make the dressing: Sauté green onions slowly in 2 tablespoons of olive oil until just tender. Stir in mushrooms and cook for 2-3 minutes; then stir in bread crumbs and cheese. Transfer mixture to a bowl and let stand until cool. Stir in white wine, lemon juice, and remaining oil. Season well with garlic salt, salt, and pepper, then add ham.
4. Prepare each artichoke by pulling out some of the center leaves until the choke can be reached. Scrape out all the hairy choke with a teaspoon.
5. Fill each artichoke with dressing and top with parsley. Wrap each artichoke in foil, leaving the top open.
6. Place on smoker grid and smoke for 1 to 1-1/2 hours.
